title: Why we should not fear fruit

chunks.title: Introduction to Fruit and Diet
chunks.summary: The speaker introduces the topic of fruit consumption, challenging the common fears associated with it. They reference studies that compare low fructose diets to those including moderate natural fructose from fruit.
chunks.transcript: why should you not fear fruit well consider these two studies which i have spoken about before but are so cool that they are worth repeating over and

chunks.title: Study Findings on Fructose Diets
chunks.summary: Discussing a randomized controlled trial, the speaker highlights that both low and moderate fructose diets led to significant weight loss and improvements in metabolic syndrome parameters. Notably, eliminating processed fructose was key to these benefits.
chunks.transcript: over and over i have done a whole podcast on this the effect of two energy restricted diets a low fructose diet versus a moderate natural fructose diet natural fructose being fruit chunks.title: Fruit Juice and Inflammation
chunks.summary: The speaker presents a study on red orange juice, which showed that its consumption can improve endothelial function and reduce inflammation in subjects with cardiovascular risk. They argue that whole fruits, as opposed to processed fructose, are beneficial for health.
chunks.transcript: controlled trial showing this and i've seen it myself in my own diet many times over i'm now eating probably 200 250 grams of carbohydrates from fruit and honey my uric acid remains below four milligrams per deciliter i do not have insulin resistance diabetes or obesity clearly my fasting insulin remains lower than three my c-peptide less than 0.5 show me any physician who's an author of that study that can claim those metrics but doubt it one more study to just quickly show you the exoneration of fruit the effects of red orange juice intake on endothelial function and inflammatory markers in adult subjects with increased cardiovascular risk many will say okay fruit is all right but don't do fruit juice well here's red orange juice that shows seven day consumption it improves endothelial function and reduces inflammation in non-diabetic subjects with increased cardiovascular risk so why is this red orange juice so bad for humans i think that basically the key here is that processed fructose is not good for humans probably too much of that ends up passing through the gut and ending up in the liver but that the gut has many mechanisms to protect the liver from excess fructose especially when it's in the form of fruit and that fruit is quite healthy for

chunks.title: Evolutionary Perspective on Diet
chunks.summary: The speaker discusses how human dietary practices align with those of our ancestors, emphasizing the natural consumption of fruits, honey, and animal products. The speaker points out that traditional diets often lacked vegetables and maintained low uric acid levels.
chunks.transcript: humans imagine that an evolutionarily consistent thing so much of this is intuitive and falls in line with what we would have done naturally as humans in the quote-unquote wild we would have sought fruit we would have eaten honey we would have killed animals and eaten them from nose to tail our uric acids would have been very low we would have been viral horny healthy and happy and there were no vegetables in sight most of the time at least not with the hadza or many other indigenous cultures that i've studied in the literature but i visited the house in person i can tell you not a lot of vegetables there at all so

chunks.title: Personal Dietary Habits
chunks.summary: The speaker shares their personal diet, which consists of organs, meat, fruit, honey, and raw dairy. They detail the quantities of different food types they consume and highlight their approach to protein intake.
chunks.transcript: do not fear fruit i'm at 250 to 200 grams a day depending how much i do you can see on my instagram and other social medias actual pictures and videos of what i eat in very clear detail i want to spell this out for people so those are the three categories of macros guys protein fat carbohydrates the foods i compose my diet of are organs meat fruit honey raw dairy i eat maybe half an ounce of liver per day a few ounces per week i think that's a optimal dose for humans i have multiple ounces of heart when i can get it i have a few ounces of testicle when i can get it i can't get it i'll use desiccated organs from heart and soil supplements those are freeze-dried and there's all kinds of good organs in there that fill in the gaps two pounds plus of meat per day like i talked about one gram of protein per pound of body weight to 1.25 grams of protein per pound of body weight i let fat happen with the meat seeking out the fattiest meat that i can get i don't fear fruit i have pineapple mango banana plantain grenada all kinds of fruit here in costa rica i'm in an equatorial zone so i'm lucky i also eat papaya and if you think it's expensive look for the video on instagram about how i do this for less than 25 a day it's not rocket science the secret is ground beef it's

chunks.title: Cost-effective Diet Strategies
chunks.summary: The speaker offers insights into maintaining a budget-friendly diet while ensuring nutritional adequacy, using ground beef as a staple. They encourage viewers to consider their dietary choices based on practicality and cost.
chunks.transcript: easy and then i have raw dairy i have maybe half a liter of raw dairy per day from raw goat's milk that i fermented a kefir
